name: Oneaz
description_short: OneAZ Credit Union is an Arizona-based credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, home equity loans, and business banking to over 12,000 local businesses and individual members.
description_long:
OneAZ Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Arizona residents and businesses. The organization operates multiple branch and ATM locations throughout Arizona and positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks. OneAZ was formerly associated with 1st Bank Yuma, which members can access through legacy login portals. The credit union has established itself as a significant player in Arizona's financial services market, emphasizing local relationships and personalized service.

OneAZ offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking products. For consumers, they provide checking and savings accounts (including a high-yield Propel Savings account at 4.00% APY up to $10,000), Share Certificates (2.85% APY on 24-month terms), auto loans (5.19% APY for 48 months on used vehicles), fixed-rate home equity loans with a 0.50% rate discount, and mortgage services. They also offer direct deposit with up to 2-day early pay access. For businesses, OneAZ provides full-service relationship banking with specialized business loan products. Members can access mobile banking with check deposit, fund transfers, bill payment, and ATM location services.

OneAZ distinguishes itself through several specific offerings: a federal tax deduction program for qualifying auto loan interest (2025-2028), a specialized assistance program for federal workers affected by government shutdowns, and competitive promotional rates on savings products. Their Propel Savings account has a tiered structure with higher APY up to $10,000. The credit union emphasizes financial literacy through their Financial Resources Guide, offering articles and calculators for credit improvement, debt payoff strategies, budgeting, and auto loan calculations.

As a credit union, OneAZ operates on a member-ownership model rather than shareholder profit maximization, which typically aligns incentives toward member benefit. However, their website provides limited detail on membership eligibility requirements, fee structures, or minimum account balances. The organization targets both individual consumers and business customers, though specific lending criteria, credit requirements, or approval processes are not detailed on their public-facing website.
services: Propel Savings high-yield savings account (4.00% APY)
Checking and savings accounts with early direct deposit (up to 2 days)
Auto loans (5.19% APY for 48 months on used vehicles)
Fixed-rate home equity loans (0.50% rate discount)
Business banking and commercial lending services
Mobile banking app (check deposit, transfers, bill pay, ATM locator)
Financial resources and education (credit guides, calculators, budgeting tools)
Auto loan tax deduction program consultation
pros: High-yield savings account offering 4.00% APY on up to $10,000 (Propel Savings)
Competitive auto loan rates at 5.19% APY for 48-month used auto loans
Federal tax deduction program for qualifying auto loan interest (2025-2028)
Up to 2-day early direct deposit access for paycheck funding
Dedicated assistance program for federal workers during government shutdowns
Full-featured mobile banking app with check deposit, bill pay, and fund transfers
Over 12,000 Arizona business customers indicating established B2B banking relationships
cons: Website does not disclose membership eligibility requirements or geographic service limitations
No transparent fee schedule or account minimum balance information published online
Limited information on credit requirements for loan approval or qualification criteria
Home equity loan rates shown only as discounts without posted base rates displayed
No details on business loan types, SBA lending, or equipment financing options
best_for: Arizona residents seeking high-yield savings with FDIC/NCUA insurance through credit union membership
Automobile buyers in Arizona looking for competitive fixed-rate auto loans
Arizona small business owners needing relationship-based commercial banking
Federal employees seeking emergency financial assistance during government service disruptions
diagnosis: OneAZ Credit Union is best for Arizona residents and business owners seeking traditional full-service banking through a member-owned credit union structure with competitive rates on savings and auto lending. The main caveat is that membership eligibility, account requirements, and detailed lending criteria are not transparently disclosed on their public website, requiring direct contact with their Member Care Center (844-663-2928) for specific qualification details.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
